title: Woven-Back Bench
summary: An outdoor bench with jigsaw-shaped 2x4 back legs and a decorative woven lattice back built from small angle-cut Y pieces — genuinely more advanced joinery than a typical farm bench.
description:
This bench's signature feature is its back: eight small angle-cut 2x2 "Y" pieces, assembled and attached in a pattern that creates a decorative woven look across the seat back — more visual interest than a typical slatted bench back, and the one part of this build that rewards patience over speed. Cut the shaped back legs first, starting the long straight cuts with a circular saw and finishing the curves with a handsaw or jigsaw; go slow with a sharp blade and sand the cut edges well, since this shaped leg is the piece everyone will see in profile.

Build the two leg assemblies — back leg, front leg, seat support, armrest support, and bottom stretcher — as matched pairs, checking that they're identical before joining them with 2x4 cross-members into a complete frame. Set the frame aside once it's done and build the back panel separately: lay all the back boards out first to confirm everything fits before assembling, since the woven Y pieces depend on consistent spacing across boards that were cut individually.

Hide pocket holes on the undersides and insides wherever the plan allows, specifically to keep water from pooling in an exposed hole outdoors — a small detail that matters more on this bench than most, given how many individual small parts make up the back. Attach the seat slats and arm rests last, both screwed from the underside so no fastener shows on a visible surface.

materials.name: Wood plugs, for exposed pocket holes

steps.title: Cut and shape the back legs
steps.body: Cut two back legs, starting the long straight cuts with a circular saw and finishing the shaped curve with a handsaw or jigsaw. Go slow with a sharp blade and sand the cut edges well.

steps.title: Build the two leg assemblies
steps.body: Attach the front legs, seat supports, armrest supports, and bottom stretchers to the back legs. Build two matched assemblies, using 1-1/2" pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue, hiding pocket holes on the underside where possible to keep water from pooling.

steps.title: Join the frame
steps.body: Join the two leg assemblies together with the 2x4 and 2x2 cross-members to complete the bench frame. Set aside.

steps.title: Build the back panel
steps.body: Lay out all the back verticals, diagonal pieces, and trim to confirm everything fits before assembling. Attach with pocket holes on the back sides for a painted finish, hiding holes on the insides of the 2x2s where the Y pieces will cover them.

steps.title: Build and attach the Y pieces
steps.body: Assemble the eight Y pieces from their short arms and stubs — these are decorative and can be secured with a good outdoor glue. Attach the Y pieces to the back panel, then attach the completed back panel to the bench frame. Fill any exposed pocket holes with plugs.
materials: Wood plugs, for exposed pocket holes

steps.title: Attach the bottom stretcher, seat slats, and armrests
steps.body: Attach the bottom stretcher to the base. Add the seat slats and top trim from the underside with 2" screws and glue to hide fasteners. Attach the armrests the same way. Sand and finish.

unresolvedImages.alt: Outdoor bench with a decorative woven lattice back
